general_notes: Values must come from the primary peer-reviewed publication of each Phase 3 trial (NEJM 2022-2024). Source the progression-free survival (PFS) Hazard Ratio and median PFS from the intention-to-treat (ITT) or all-randomized-patients analysis — do NOT use pre-specified subgroup analyses (e.g., HR+ vs HR- in DESTINY-Breast04) or subsequent updated analyses. The PFS HR is the hazard ratio for the primary PFS comparison, treatment arm vs control arm. Median PFS is reported for the experimental/treatment arm only. These trials all used RECIST 1.1 criteria for PFS. Citations should include the primary NEJM paper (not interim analyses published separately, not subgroup papers).

search_groups.description: Trial name with drug name and tumor type/indication.

Progression-free survival outcomes from landmark Phase 3 trials published in NEJM 2022-2024. Requires identifying the correct primary publication, locating the ITT/all-patients PFS analysis, and extracting the hazard ratio and median PFS for the experimental arm. Key pitfalls: (1) subgroup HR differs from ITT HR — use ITT; (2) OS HR differs from PFS HR — use PFS; (3) some trials had multiple interim publications before the primary paper — use the primary NEJM paper; (4) median PFS is for the treatment arm only, not control or combined.

validation_targets.description: Trial name, investigational drug, and indication.
validation_targets.notes: Use the format 'TRIAL-NAME (drug / indication)' as given.
examples: LAURA (osimertinib / unresectable stage III NSCLC after CRT)
KEYNOTE-811 (pembrolizumab / HER2-positive gastric or GEJ cancer)

validation_targets.description: Progression-free survival hazard ratio (HR), experimental arm vs control, from the primary ITT analysis of each trial's primary NEJM publication.
validation_targets.notes: Source: primary NEJM publication, ITT or all-randomized population, PFS endpoint. HR <1 favors experimental arm. Express to 2 decimal places. Do NOT use OS HR, subgroup PFS HR, or updated analysis HR. DESTINY-Breast04: HR is for all-randomized patients (HR+ and HR- combined), not the pre-specified primary HR+ subgroup. LAURA: HR reflects the dramatic treatment effect of osimertinib vs placebo. KEYNOTE-811: HR from the second interim analysis (primary NEJM 2023 publication). FLAURA2: HR for osimertinib+chemo vs osimertinib monotherapy. ADRIATIC: HR for durvalumab vs placebo.

validation_targets.description: Median progression-free survival in months for the experimental (investigational) arm only, from the same primary ITT analysis.
validation_targets.notes: Report for the treatment arm only (T-DXd, osimertinib, pembrolizumab, osimertinib+chemo, or durvalumab arm as appropriate). Do NOT report control arm or combined median. Source: same primary NEJM publication, same ITT analysis population. Round to 1 decimal place.
